verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Petilianus said: "For everything consists of an origin and root; and if it have not something for a head, it is nothing: nor does anything well receive second birth, unless it be born again of good seed."

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Augustin answered: Why will you put yourself forward in the room of Christ, when you will not place yourself under Him?

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: He is the origin, and root, and head of him who is being born, and in Him we feel no fear, as we must in any man, whoever he may be, lest he should prove to be false and of abandoned character, and we should be found to be sprung from an abandoned source, growing from an abandoned root, united to an abandoned head.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: For what man can feel secure about a man, when it is written, "Cursed be the man that trusteth in man?"

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: But the seed of which we are born again is the word of God, that is, the gospel.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Whence the apostle says, "For in Christ Jesus I have begotten you through the gospel."

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: And yet he allows even those to preach the gospel who were preaching it not in purity, and rejoices in their preaching; because, although they were preaching it not in purity, but seeking their own, not the things which are Jesus Christ’s, yet the gospel which they preached was pure.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: And the Lord had said of certain of like character, "Whatsoever they bid you observe, that observe and do; but do not yet after their works: for they say, and do not."

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: If, therefore, what is in itself pure is preached in purity, then the preacher himself also, in that he is a partner with the word, has his share in begetting the believer; but if he himself be not regenerate, and yet what he preaches be pure, then the believer is born not from the barrenness of the minister but from the fruitfulness of the word.
